mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal an extended zero-lower-bound monetary regime that suppresses real discount rates and increases equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS remains in the ultra-low regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-04-15 indicates sustained zero-rate environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires sustained positive employment flow.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200,000 persons for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,848 thousand as of 2015-04-15 indicates ongoing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with sustained job creation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following two consecutive months of PAYEMS growth > 200k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 600,000 annualized units, signaling strong residential investment demand and forward-looking builder confidence. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as housing strength feeds into construction employment, materials demand, and consumer wealth effects.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 590,000 units as of 2015-03-15 is near the trigger threshold
regime_hint: housing recovery phase with improving construction activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when HOUST > 600k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in housing-strong regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines more than 1.5% over a 3-month rolling window, signaling a contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and forward economic weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.46 as of 2015-03-15 shows near-trend levels
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following INDPRO declines > 1.5% over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal demand-side momentum when both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because synchronized consumer and residential demand indicates broad-based economic expansion that supports equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344,688 million and HOUST at 590,000 units as of 2015-03-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST grow month-over-month, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.4 percentage points over a 2-month window, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and forward earnings risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity markets reprice corporate profit expectations downward in response to weakening consumer purchasing power.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.4 percentage points over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% as of 2015-04-15 is near post-crisis lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances following UNRATE increases > 0.4pp over 2 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) interact such that when inflation rises above 2.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, the real policy rate becomes deeply negative, compressing equity risk premiums. This regime-conditional interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in this specific monetary-inflation regime.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.79 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-03-15
regime_hint: negative real rates regime with moderate inflation and ultra-low nominal rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when real rates are deeply negative (inflation > 2.5%, FEDFUNDS < 0.5%), or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter exceeds 2.5% annualized, signaling sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when the economy is in above-trend growth regimes, as higher GDP growth translates directly to higher corporate profit growth.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,014 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2015-01-15 indicates ongoing expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window during expansion periods.
